channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pakistan Records $479 Million Surplus in February Current Account
item.description: Pakistan’s current account has seen a welcome turnaround, posting a surplus of $479 million in February, according to the latest figures from the State Bank of Pakistan (SBP). This is a significant sh...

item.title: House Votes to Eliminate Travel Tax for Filipino Travelers
item.description: MANILA, Philippines – The House of Representatives approved a bill on March 16, 2026, seeking to abolish the travel tax imposed on Filipino travelers for international flights. House Bill No. 8464, wh...

item.title: Delhi Assembly Speaker Supports Om Birla Amid Opposition Motion
item.description: Delhi Assembly Speaker Vijender Gupta expressed strong disapproval of the recent opposition move in the Lok Sabha aimed at removing Speaker Om Birla. In a letter addressed to Birla, Gupta emphasised t...

item.title: Kerala Man Killed by Neighbour in Suspected Revenge Case
item.description: In a tragic incident at Manjapra near Angamaly, Kerala, a 50-year-old man named Jose Chakku, also known as Kilukkan Jose, was allegedly shot dead by his neighbour, Pauly Jose, on the evening of March ...

item.title: Court rules parliamentary staff dismissals over certificates were unfair, orders Sh12 million payout
item.description: Six parliamentary employees who were dismissed over allegations of presenting forged academic certificates have won a total of Sh12 million in damages after the Employment and Labour Relations Court f...

item.title: The Brontë Sisters' Novels Ranked from Worst to Best
item.description: This Guardian article ranks the seven published novels by the Brontë sisters — Charlotte, Emily, and Anne — from least to most accomplished, prompted by renewed interest due to Emerald Fennell’s recen...

item.title: Helicopter Crash In Tema Community 1 Kills Two, Authorities Investigating
item.description: Two people don die after one helicopter crash for near Oninku Park, Tema Community 1, Ghana. Security people and aviation authorities rush go the scene sharp sharp, cordon off the area and start preli...

item.title: Fashion Highlights from the 2026 Vanity Fair Oscars After-Party
item.description: Following the 2026 Academy Awards, the Vanity Fair after-party at the Los Angeles County Museum of Art showcased a dramatic shift from traditional red carpet glamour to daring, sensual fashion. Celebr...

item.title: 20th Television Animation Begins Animated Firefly Series with Nathan Fillion
item.description: An animated version of the cult-favorite science fiction series Firefly is in early development at 20th Television Animation, with actor Nathan Fillion returning to participate in the project. The reb...

item.title: Analysis of Canada’s Economic Performance Under Carney’s First Year as Prime Minister
item.description: Mark Carney’s first year as Canada’s prime minister has been marked by economic challenges, despite his campaign promises of improvement. Unemployment has remained high, with February alone seeing a l...

item.title: Charming Café in Mudgee Known for Its Jaffles and All-Day Menu
item.description: Tucked away in a cobblestone alley, Alby and Esthers is a delightful café that embodies the charm of Mudgee’s heritage. Originally named by the café's previous owners, the name was retained by Mudgee ...

item.title: CBI Faces Staff Shortage; Parliamentary Panel Recommends Reserve List to Fill Vacancies
item.description: A parliamentary panel has raised concerns over persistent staff shortages in the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI), pointing out that the agency is currently operating with a 14.90% deficit of off...

item.title: How Nigerians Fit Use Naira Cards for International Payments
item.description: Nigerians don dey able to use their Naira cards for international payments again. Before now, if you wan pay for something abroad, you go need plan well: you fit open virtual USD card, fund domiciliar...

item.title: Ghana to evacuate its citizens from Qatar amid regional tensions
item.description: The Ghanaian government don decide to carry out emergency evacuation for its nationals wey dey live for Qatar. Dis na precautionary measure because Iran dey continue with drone and missile attacks acr...

item.title: Lok Sabha to Discuss Revocation of Suspension of Eight Opposition MPs on March 17
item.description: In a significant political development, the suspension of eight Opposition Members of Parliament (MPs) is expected to be revoked in the upcoming session of the Lok Sabha on 17th March. The suspended M...

item.title: Student Tangai Mugo Builds Photography Venture from Childhood Passion During Lockdown
item.description: During the COVID-19 lockdown, when schools closed and everyone stayed home, Tangai Mugo, a student at Crawford International School, went back to his childhood love for photography. He started by usin...

item.title: Challenges in Securing the Strait of Hormuz Amid Iran-U.S. Tensions
item.description: Da Strait of Hormuz, wey dey between Iran and Oman, na very important waterway because about one-fifth of global oil and gas dey pass there. President Donald Trump don demand support from allied count...

item.title: How online business models contribute to children’s exposure to harmful content
item.description: Children are increasingly exposed to harmful online content, including violent, sexual, and self-harm material. While this is often viewed as a failure of moderation, the root cause is largely economi...

item.title: Paul Ehrlich: Environmental Scientist and Advocate for Public Awareness
item.description: Paul Ehrlich, a prominent Stanford biologist who passed away on March 15, 2026, was a leading voice in environmental science, renowned for his warnings about population growth, resource scarcity, and ...

item.title: Meningitis Outbreak in Kent: Symptoms, Treatment, and Vaccination Guidance
item.description: Authorities in Kent, UK, have issued warnings to students following the deaths of a University of Kent student and a Year 13 pupil from Queen Elizabeth’s Grammar School due to meningitis. The UK Healt...
